paragraphs.text: In the tension that modern life presents to us, the problem of managing time is very important. I am not going to try to convince him that he has plenty of time and can pray if he wants; I want to talk about managing time amid tensions, the agitación de la vida. No le voy a explicar cómo conseguir tiempo: I will only say that if we try to lose a little less, we will have more. If we use the crumbs of the time we waste to obtain small moments of retreat and prayer, we will discover that there are great amounts of time. If you think about the empty minutes at day, when we are only doing something because it scares us being alone with ourselves, we will realize that there are many minutes that can belong to us and to God at the same time time. But what I want to talk about is something that I consider more important. It is the way we can control and stop time.

We can only pray to God if we find ourselves in a state of stability and inner peace face to face with God; those things set us free of the sense of time —not time as something objective, the one that we measure with the clock—, but rather the subjective feeling that it runs to at great speed and it escapes us.

First of all, I would like you to notice something that we all know and We comment. There is no need to run after time to catch it. No escape from us, run towards us. Whether you are paying attention to next minute or completely distracted, will come in your direction.
The future, whatever you do about it, will become the present, so no hay necesidad de tratar de saltar desde el presente al futuro.

We can simply wait for him/her to come, and in that sense, we can be completely serene and yet move to time, because it is time that moves. You know what it happens when you are in a car or on a train and are sitting without driving, looking out the window; can read, can think, can to rest and, however, the train moves and, in a way, what the future will appear, whether its destination is the next station 41

as if it is the last. I think this is very important. The mistake that Many times we make mistakes in our inner life by imagining that if we hurry we will be in the future soon - a bit like the man who runs from the last car of the train to the first, hoping that the distance between London and Edinburgh will be reduced from that way. With that kind of examples we see how absurd it is, but when we try to live an inch ahead of ourselves not we perceive the absurdity of the effort. However, this is what we prevents being fully in the present moment, that I dare to say it is the only moment we can be, not even with the imagination we can get ahead of time. The only thing that happens is we are in a hurry, but we do not move faster because of it. It they have had to check more than once. Someone loaded with two very heavy suitcases trying to catch a bus, starts running hurriedly: he goes as fast as the suitcases allow him and is full of effort to be where it is not.
But you know what happens when we go on vacation.

We can walk quickly, happily, with energy or, if we have the age and appropriate conditions, we can even run, but we do not we do not feel rushed at all, because what matters in those moments is the race not the arrival. This is what we have to learn about the sentence, put ourselves in the present. From ordinary, we think or behave as if the present were a truly very fine imaginary line, between the past and the future, and we roll from the past to the future continuously crossing this line, how an egg can roll over a piece of fabric. If it does so, it rolls continuously, it is not in any place at a moment determined, without present, because it is always in the future.
Not everyone is as fortunate as to have lived decisive, enlightening experiences, but I would like to tell you briefly a very useful experience that I had.

During the occupation of France by the Germans, I belonged to resistance movement and once I went down to the subway, I was stopped by the police. This was one of the most interesting experiences I have had. Setting aside the romantic aspect of what happened and how it happened, I will underline in philosophical terms what relates to the time. What happened at that moment was this: I had a past and a future, and I moved from one to the other by 42

go down the stairs. At one point someone put a hand on the shoulder and said: "Stop, give me your documentation." In at that moment several things happened. One of them is that I began to to think very quickly and to feel with great intensity and to realize of the situation with a clarity and lucidity that had never been experienced, on the last steps of the subway station Star. The second thing was that I understood I had no past, because the the real past I had was what could take me to the wall of firing squad. So that past had disappeared. The false the past that I was prepared to talk about was not there I had never existed in such a way that I found myself there like the lizard that has been caught by the tail and escaped leaving the tail in somewhere back, so the lizard ends where the tail was.

Then I discovered another thing that was very interesting (although I did not I thought so much about the philosophical aspect of time at that moment, but what I noticed right away and gradually understood is that you have a future only to the extent that you can prever un minuto antes de que suceda o un centímetro antes de having reached it, what is going to happen; for example, nothing happens because you have no idea what could happen; you are like someone who is in an unknown room, in the dark.

You are there and all that exists is darkness weighing upon you. eyes. There may be nothing or an infinite in front of you, it is Same. You end where darkness begins. Thus I discovered that it also had no future. It was then that I discovered that to live in the past, on one hand, and in the future, on the other hand, simply, it was not possible. The lizard had no tail and the darkness I covered my face. I discovered that I was trapped at that moment. present and all my past, that is, everything I could be, had been condensed in the present moment with an intensity, a colorful extraordinarily stimulating and eventually allowed me Escape!

Now, as for the weather, there are times, without going too far detail, in which one can perceive that the present moment is there, the past has irremediably vanished -- it has no importance except insofar as it is still in the present -- and the The future doesn't matter because it may or may not happen. This happens, for example, when you have an accident, when you find yourself in a moment of danger that requires quick action. You do not it has time to roll smoothly from the past to the future. What it has 43

what to do is to be so completely in the present that all your energies and all your being be concentrated on the word 'now'. You know the thin plane that geometry teaches us, which does not have absolutely body. That geometric plane, which has no body in absolute, what is "now", moves along the timeline, or more Well, time runs beneath him and brings you "now" everything what you will need in the future; This is the situation we must to learn, and we must learn it in a more pleasant way. I think that we must do exercises to stop time and stay in the present, in this "now" that is my present and that is also a intersection of eternity with time.

What can we do? This is the first exercise. It can be done in moments when I have absolutely nothing to do, when nothing pushes him forward or backward or when he can emplear cinco minutos, tres minutos o media hora de ocio y de no do nothing. You sit and say: 'I am sitting. I am not doing anything.' nothing. I will do nothing for five minutes" and then he calms down and constantly during this time (one or two minutes is the most that can you hold it to begin) it is said: "I am here in the presence of God, in my own presence and in the presence of the furniture that I surrounds me, quiet, without moving me." There is, of course, one more thing that should decide that in the space of those two minutes, five minutes, which has been assigned to learn that the present he will not be distracted by the phone, a knock at the door or a sudden burst of energy that drives him to do right away what has stopped doing for the last ten years. So you Install and say: 'Here I am' and you are there. If you learn to do this in lost moments of his life, when he has learned not to be nervous inside, but to be completely calm, happy, stable and serene, then extend the few minutes for a period greater and then by another even longer. There will come a moment, of course, you will need some defenses, because it can you stay still for a few minutes even with the phone ringing or if someone knocks on the door, while fifteen minutes can be too many for the phone to ring or to keep someone waiting person at the door. But then think that if I were not at home, I wouldn't open the door or answer the phone. Or, if it takes more courage or he is more convinced of what he is doing, he can do the same what my father did. He put a small note on the door saying: 'No Please do not hesitate to call. I am at home, but I will not open. 44
more decisive system, because people understand it right away, while if you say: 'Please wait five minutes', the 'please' it usually ends in two minutes.

Then, when you have reached this stability, this serenity, you will have to learn to stop time not only in moments when it flows slowly or has stopped, but at moments when it seems gallop. To "do it, one must act this way. You are doing something that you consider useful: think that unless it is donethis, the world will stop rotating in its orbit and then, if at a certain the moment you say: 'I stop', you will discover many things. First, you will discover that the world does not stop and that everyone -- if they can you imagine -- can: wait five minutes while you leave to take care of him. This is important because we generally we deceive ourselves, saying: "Well, I must do it: it's charity, it's a duty." I can't leave it undone." You can, because in moments of extreme laziness will leave it undone for more time than the five minutes you have chosen. So the first thing he says is: "Let it be what sea, whatever happens, I stop here. The simplest thing to do It's to set an alarm clock. Wind it up and say: "Now I'm working" "without looking at the clock until it rings." This is very important; one of the One of the things we must learn is to look at the clock. If you are walking. he is going somewhere and is convinced that it has gotten late, You look at the watch. But you cannot walk so quickly. while looking at the watch on his wrist as if simply looking towards go ahead. And if you realize that it has been seven, five or three minutes delayed, for the case it is the same. So leave earlier and Will he arrive on time or if he is delayed, walk as fast and energetically as possible. as much as possible. When you reach the door, put on a face of regret so that it can be seen when the door opens! Then, when it rings the alarm clock, you know that during the next five minutes the the world has ended and will not move from its place. It is God's time and you are sitting in your time quietly, silently and in peace. At first, you will notice how difficult it is, and you will feel that you have a lot importance of finishing a letter or reading a paragraph. In reality, soon you will find that it can be delayed by five to even ten minutes and not it's okay. And if you are doing something that requires attention, you will discover that you can do it much better and faster.
I'll give you another example. At first, when I was a doctor, it seemed to me that it was unfair to the people who were in the waiting room to dedicate 45

a long time to the person who was in my consultation. So the the first time I tried to be as quick as possible during the consultation. I discovered at the end of the day, I had not kept the slightest memory of the people I had seen because during all the time that a the patient was with me, I looked beyond their person with eyes the fortuneteller headed towards the waiting room and counted the heads of those who they were yet to enter. As a result, all the questions I asked, the I had to repeat all the explorations I did twice.

I had to do it two or three times. When I finished, I couldn't remember. whether I had done it or not. Of course, not everyone is like me; you may be able to remember much better than I do, but this is just an example of what could happen even to you.
Then I thought that it was not honorable, and I decided that I was going to act. as if the person who was with me were the only one that existed.

At the moment I started to feel 'I must hurry', I I sat down again and engaged in a trivial conversation for a few minutes to avoid getting rushed. I discovered, after two days, that you don't need to do anything like that. It's enough for you to be interested. fully on the person or task you have in front of you and when you have finished, you will realize that you have spent half the time what you used to employ before; however, you have seen everything and listened everything.

Since then I have often given this type of advice to many people from various social levels and serves. So if you do those exercises, starting by stopping the time that does not move and ending with the time that tries to move very quickly, and you se para y dice: «No», descubrirá que desde el momento en que ha dominated the internal tension, the internal agitation, the restlessness and the anguish, time passes perfectly well. Can you imagine that Does only one minute pass every minute? This is exactly what happens.

It's strange, but it's true, although by the way we... one might think that five minutes could pass unrestrained in thirty seconds. No, every minute is worth the same that the following, every hour, like the following. Nothing happens disaster. One might ask: "Will I have enough time to do everything?" He I will answer in a very Russian style: "If he doesn't die first, he will have time to do it. If he dies before, he won't need to do it. another saying of the same kind that you may remember as future reference: "Don't worry about death. When death comes, you " 46
"You are no longer here, but while you are, death does not exist." It is the same. principle. Why am I going to worry about a situation that is Will it resolve on its own?

Once you have learned not to worry, you will be able to do anything. thing, at any speed, with any level of attention and speed, without feeling that time is slipping away or that it it overwhelms. It is like the feeling I have talked about, when it is on vacation, with all the vacation ahead. You can act slowly or quickly, without a sense of time, because you are dependent only on what is being done, without a purpose determined. And then you will see that you can pray anywhere the world's situation, that there is no situation that can prevent it to pray. What may prevent you from praying is that you yourself join in at the center of the whirlwind or allow the whirlwind to invade you instead of keep it out of oneself.

You may remember the story of the storm on the Sea of Galilee, in the Gospel: Christ, sleeping in the boat, and the storm raging around. At first, the apostles toiled hard and with confidence to survive. Then, at a certain moment, they lost hope, and the storm that was outside entered them inside -- the storm was within him, then. The anguish, the death does not simply circulate around, it penetrates within. And then they turn to Christ and do what we very we often do with God: we look to God in times of hurry and tragedy and it angers us that He is so calm. The story of the Gospel emphasizes that Christ was sleeping with the head on a pillow, for greater insult. They were at point of dying and He was resting comfortably. This is exactly what we often feel about God. How he dares to be carefree, how dare he be so comfortable while I am in trouble? And the disciples do exactly what we often do. Instead of addressing God and say: 'You are the peace, you are the Lord, say a word and my the created will be healed, say a word and everything will be fixed," they told him. they wake from their sleep and say: 'Aren't you concerned that we perish?' Other words: "If you can't do anything, at least don't sleep. If you can't do anything better, at least die anguished like we». Christ reacts, stands up and says: "Men of little "Hey!" and he removes them from his side, turns towards the storm, projecting 47
your inner calm, your harmony and peace in the storm; says: "Calm yourself, "detente," and everything calms down again.
We can do this, and we must be able to do it.

But it requires systematic and intelligent preparation, of the same. the way we prepare to do other things. Learn to master time and whatever happens, in any difficulty, in the storm, in the tragedy or simply in the midst of confusion in that we continuously live, may be calm, still in the present, face to face with the Lord, in silence or with words. If he uses words can bring God everything that surrounds him, all the confusion.

If you are in silence, you can rest in the 'eye of the cyclone' or of hurricane, in the calm, but allowing the violence of the storm burst around you, while you are where God is, in the unique point of total stability. But that point of total stability it is not a point where nothing happens. It is the point where all the tensions in conflict converge and find a counterbalance some in others and are held by the powerful hand of God.

Real silence is sometimes extremely intense, it has density and he is truly alive. I remember a passage from the life of the saints desert fathers (hermits), in which one of them received a request from his brothers to deliver a sermon during the the stay of a bishop who was going to visit them, and he said: 'No, I don't want to do it because if my silence doesn't speak to him, my words will be useless.
This is the kind of silence that we should try to learn. How do it? What I can try to guide you is to propose a parable or an image, that of observing birds.

If we want to see the birds moving in the forests or in the Fields, we have to wake up earlier than them. We must prepare ourselves. to be vigilant, alert, clear-headed before the first bird wake up. Truly, before the birds realize that the morning has arrived. We must go to the field or the forest and remain absolutely still, in absolute silence, absolutely relaxed, so that we do nothing movement so that light sleepers do not wake up animals that are around us, because if not, they will escape towards the distance, and they will fly to where we cannot hear or see them. The observation of the birds implies, on one side, this immobility, this stillness, this rest, and at the same time, an intense surveillance, because if he sits 48
you in the field and start daydreaming about what you have not I could dream at night all the birds will have disappeared before that you realize the sun is warming your back.

It is essential to be alert and clear-headed, while at the same time calm and still. calm, and this is a contemplative preparation for silence contemplative; this difficult balance between the kind of alertness that will allow him to have a completely open mind, free of prejudices, in a waiting attitude, to receive the impact of any something that comes to meet you, and, at the same time, this stillness that it will allow you to receive the impact without introducing the image of your own presence that would destroy it.

Twenty years ago, shortly after my ordination, I was sent, before of Christmas, at the home of an old woman. There lived a lady who died Some time later, at the age of one hundred and two years, he came to see me. after I celebrated my first mass, and said: 'Father, I would like to that he could give me some advice about prayer." So I said to him: "Oh, Very well, I asked about Fulanito de tal." She said: "All these years I have been asking people who are reputed to understand. about the prayer, and they have never given me a sensible answer, so I have thought that since you probably know nothing, perhaps I can find the correct answer. What a nice situation! So I I said to her: "What is your problem?" The elderly woman said: "The last fourteen For years I have been reciting the prayer of Jesus almost continuously and "I have never noticed the presence of God." Then, a little to blind, I said to him: 'If you talk all the time, you do not leave God occasion to pronounce a word." She said: "What should I do?" I said: "Go to your room after breakfast, put it in order, place your chair in a strategic position that leaves your back all the corners where discarded things remain that does not want to be seen. Turn on your table lamp in front of the icon that you have and first of all, take inventory of your room.

Stay seated, look around and try to see where you live, because I am sure that if you have prayed continuously those fourteen Years, it has been a long time since you last saw your room. And then take out your knitting and for fifteen minutes do some stitching in front of God; but I forbid you to say a single word of prayer. You do it. point and tried to enjoy the peace of your room.
She didn't think it was very pious advice, but she followed it.
After a little while, he came to see me and said: "Do you know? It is 49

effective." I said: What is effective, what is happening?" Because I felt full of curiosity to know how my advice worked. And she said: 'I I did what you advised me to do. I get up, I wash myself, I tidy up my room, have breakfast, return, I make sure that There is nothing left for me to worry about, and then I sit in my armchair. and I think: Oh, how pleasant, I spend fifteen minutes in which I can "not doing anything without feeling guilty!" And I look around and, first time after many years, I think: "My God, how beautiful" I have a room with a window open to the garden, a nice one. disposition, enough space for me—, how many things I have gathered in these years." Then she said: "I felt calm because the room it was so cozy. The ticking of a clock could be heard, but it was not it distorted the silence, its sound merely underscored the fact that everything was so still and, after a while, I remembered that I had to to make a point before the face of God, so I started to weave. And I made myself more and more aware of the silence. The needles brushed against the arm of the armchair, the clock was ticking peacefully, there was nothing for which to worry, I didn't have to feel tense, and then I realized that that it was not simply an absence of noise, but that the silence had a substance. It was not the absence of something, but the presence. of something. The silence had a density, a richness that began to impregnate me. The reigning silence began to approach and merge with my silence." And in the end, he said something very beautiful that I have found more afternoon in a French writer, Georges Bernanos. He said: 'Suddenly, I perceived that silence was a presence. In the heart of silence He was there, who is all calm, peace, elegance.

After that, she lived about ten more years, and always said that she could find calm and silence when she herself I felt calm and silent. That doesn't mean I abandoned the sentence, means that I could maintain that contemplative silence for some time, then her mind started to tremble, and I returned to the vocal prayer until the mind calmed down again, then he would once again immerse himself in the wordless world of silence, like before. Very often this can happen to us, if in place to do things with full attention, we would simply say: 'God' Mine, I am in your presence, what joy, let's stay like this, calm.

In the life of a French Catholic priest, the Holy Curé of Ars, John Maria Vianney, there is a story about an old farmer who spent his time hours and hours sitting in the chapel without moving, without doing anything. The 50
The priest said to him, "What are you doing for so many hours?" The old farmer replied: I am looking at him. He looks at me and we are happy.
This can only be achieved if we learn a variety of silences.

Starting with the silence of the lips, the silence of the emotions, the silence of the mind, the silence of the body. But it would be an error to imagine that we can start from the last extreme, with the silence of the heart and the mind. We must begin by putting silence on our lips, to put silence on our body, in the the sense of learning to keep it still, to strip it of tensions, not to fall into daydreams or negligence, but to use the formula of one of our Russian saints: to be like the string of a violin, thus way to hit the right note, without being so tense, until the extreme of bursting, nor so little as to make a sound imperfect. And from that moment on, we must learn to listen in silence, to be absolutely still, and we will be able to, more often what we think, discovering that the words of the Book from the Revelation are true: 'I stand at the door and knock.' In the next chapter, we will consider the basic conditions for establish the prayer in relation to the way of addressing God and to be able to talk to him.
